data.title: Agree Realty Q3 earnings, revenue beat as investment activity increases
data.description:
Agree Realty (NYSE:ADC) boosted the lower end of its full-year earnings guidance after Q3 earnings beat the average analyst estimate by a penny.

"We have accelerated investment activity while strengthening our balance sheet through proactive capital markets transactions," said President and CEO Joey Agree.

The retail REIT raised the lower end of its 2024 adjusted FFO per share guidance by a penny to $4.12 from $4.11 and kept the upper end of its range at $4.14. The midpoint of $4.13 compares with the consensus estimate of $4.10.

General and administrative expenses are expected to be 5.7%-5.9% of adjusted revenue, compared with its previous guidance of 5.7%-6.0%.

The company expects acquisition volume of ~$850M vs. its prior view of ~$700M. Disposition volume was changed to $70M-$100M from $60M-$100M previously.

Q3 adjusted FFO per share of $1.03, topping the $1.02 consensus estimate, slipped from $1.04 in Q2 and increased from $1.00 in last year's Q3.

Revenue of $154.3M, beating the $153.9M consensus, increased from $152.6M in the prior quarter and $136.8M a year ago.

Q3 operating expenses of $81.7M climbed from $77.8M in Q2 and $73.7M in Q3 2023.

As of Sept. 30, 2024, Agree's (ADC) portfolio consisted of 2,271 properties in 49 states and contained 47.2M square feet of gross leasable area. The portfolio was 99.6% leased at Sept. 30 vs. 99.8% at June 30, had a weighted average remaining lease term of 7.9 years, and generated 67.5% of annualized base rents from investment-grade retail tenants.

Its total acquisition volume for the quarter was $215.6M and included 66 properties net leased to retailers in sectors including general merchandise, warehouse clubs, home improvement, auto parts, tire and auto service, and grocery stores. It sold two properties for gross proceeds of $7.2M in Q3.

Conference call on Oct. 23 at 9:00 AM ET.

Agree Realty (ADC) stock was little changed at $75.11 in Tuesday after-hours trading.

Earlier, Agree Realty FFO of $1.03 beats by $0.01, revenue of $154.33M beats by $0.46M

data.title: CoStar group to buy Visual Lease, a lease administration and accounting platform
data.description:
• CoStar Group (NASDAQ:CSGP) announced that it has reached a definitive agreement to acquire Visual Lease.
  • Visual Lease will become part of the CoStar Real Estate Manager platform, expanding both platforms’ product lines and extending solutions for their customers.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Texas Instruments GAAP EPS of $1.47 beats by $0.10, revenue of $4.15B beats by $30M
data.description:
• Texas Instruments press release (NASDAQ:TXN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.47 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $4.15B (-8.4% Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • "Revenue decreased 8% from the same quarter a year ago and increased 9% sequentially. Industrial continued to decline sequentially, while all other end markets grew.
  • "TI's fourth quarter outlook is for revenue in the range of $3.70 billion to $4.00 billion vs $4.08B consensus and earnings per share between $1.07 and $1.29 vs $1.41 consensus. We continue to expect our fourth quarter effective tax rate to be about 13%."

data.title: Ryan Choi, associate of Andrew Left, to pay $1.8M to settle SEC charges
data.description:
• Andrew Left associate Ryan Choi agreed to pay more than $1.8M to settle charges that he negligently engaged in a scheme that misled readers of Citron Research in connection with two tweets published by the platform, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission said on Tuesday.
  • Andrew Left, who operates the Citron Research website and related platforms, was charged in July 2024 for publishing false and misleading statements regarding his supposed stock trading recommendations.
  • The SEC complaint alleged that Choi worked with Left in December 2020 on research and content for two buy recommendations that Left issued through Citron. Choi is accused of failing to conduct adequate research or due diligence that he provided to Left to support the recommendations.
  • The SEC also alleged that Choi traded on the price increases that came after the two Citron Research tweets were published and negligently failed to disclose his trading activity in the tweets. As a result, Choi made almost $1.65M in profits from the trading.
  • Without admitting or denying the allegations in the complaint, Choi agreed to pay a civil penalty of $115K, disgorgement of $1.55M, and prejudgment interest of $64.8K.
